summary: Iteration 6 frontend tests: folder rename via context menu, span-vs-input render modes, back-arrow returning to parent folder, and breadcrumb-up disabled-at-root behavior all verified end-to-end via Playwright on public URL.
passed: Breadcrumb-up button renders at root with disabled=True and class contains opacity-30 (cursor-not-allowed)
After add-folder-btn, folder name renders as <span> (not <input>) with Tailwind 'select-text' class
Right-click on folder card opens context menu exposing data-testid='ctx-rename-folder-New Folder'
Clicking Rename swaps span -> <input> (autoFocused) at same testid 'folder-name-New Folder'; typing 'PartyA' + Enter exits rename mode and span shows 'PartyA'
Inside PartyA folder, breadcrumb-up is enabled (disabled=False)
After creating a character inside PartyA, clicking it opens inventory; back-to-roster returns to roster with breadcrumb='ROOT > PartyA' (PartyA preserved, NOT reset to root)
From inside PartyA, clicking breadcrumb-up navigates back to ROOT
notes: Drag-drop path for moving Corvo into PartyA was not exercised; instead the back-arrow flow was validated by creating a character directly inside the renamed PartyA folder, which exercises the same App.js initialFolderId={activeCharacter.parentId} -> CharacterSelect folderPath walk. Regression flows (stack +/-, quality dialog, drag-reorder, paste-bar) were NOT re-tested in this latency-sensitive pass — defer to full QA.
